intro: Today we are ranking Kale as a vegetable, per 100g, so you can see where it really lands.

sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 0.9g. this is a real omega 3 flex. for a vegetable, clean nutrient density is the real test.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 0.9g. this is a real omega 3 flex. for a vegetable, clean nutrient density is the real test.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 8.8g. fibre is seriously strong. for a vegetable, low downside and high payoff is what you want.
sections.subtitleText: carbs is 8.8g. fibre is seriously strong. for a vegetable, low downside and high payoff is what you want.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 4.3g. bioavailability is kind of absurd. for a vegetable, you want real micronutrient value, not just green vibes.
sections.subtitleText: protein is 4.3g. bioavailability is kind of absurd. for a vegetable, you want real micronutrient value, not just green vibes.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin k at 570% DV, vitamin c at 80% DV stand out most.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin k at 570% DV, vitamin c at 80% DV stand out most.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: calcium at 11% DV, potassium at 7% DV stand out most. the rest of the section is nothing special.
sections.subtitleText: calcium at 11% DV, potassium at 7% DV stand out most. the rest of the section is nothing special.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: biggest pros here. micronutrient density is ridiculous. easy to build meals around. good volume for the calories.

displayItems.title: micronutrient density is ridiculous
displayItems.explanation: This is the kind of vegetable profile that can clearly carry a category.

displayItems.title: easy to build meals around
displayItems.explanation: A practical plus because good foods need realistic use cases.

displayItems.title: good volume for the calories
displayItems.explanation: That matters for satiety and general usefulness.

sections.subtitleText: biggest pros here. micronutrient density is ridiculous. easy to build meals around. good volume for the calories.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: biggest cons here. energy density is very low. usually needs pairing to make a full meal. freshness and prep can matter.

displayItems.title: energy density is very low
displayItems.explanation: Good for some use cases, but not enough on its own.

displayItems.title: usually needs pairing to make a full meal
displayItems.explanation: Most vegetables are not complete by themselves.

displayItems.title: freshness and prep can matter
displayItems.explanation: Practical friction is still a real downside.

sections.subtitleText: biggest cons here. energy density is very low. usually needs pairing to make a full meal. freshness and prep can matter.

closing.summary: So overall, Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
closing.useCaseNote: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 59 from a base nutrition score of 56. Context lifted it by 3 points.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
